content: User's name is Nick
Lives in Hermanus, South Africa — it's home for them
Tends to stay indoors rather than go outside, and feels they don't do enough with their time
Currently watching One Piece and working on several projects
Nick watches One Piece
Nick gets frustrated when the assistant asks about things it should already know or remember
Nick's wife is named Tanya
Nick is building Ignis a pixel art room/home with interactive animations, including locations like a desk, couch, bookshelf, fireplace, clock table, kitchen, plant, front door, and window
Nick is watching One Piece on Netflix, currently around episode 157 (Skypiea arc)
Nick's wife is named Tanya, who makes him lunch and is described as 'best wife'
Nick dislikes vegetables and is a picky eater; his favourite lunch snacks are chicken nuggets with fries (ranch and Aromat) and mini pizzas, both made in the airfryer

Nick is building a pixel art interactive room/home for Ignis, with locations like couch, desk, bookshelf, fireplace, clock table, kitchen, plant, front door, and window — a passion project he actively works on and cares about
Nick's wife is named Tanya, who cooks for him and accommodates his food preferences
Nick dislikes vegetables and is a picky eater; his favourite lunch snacks are chicken nuggets with fries (ranch and Aromat) and mini pizzas, both made in the airfryer
Nick is building Ignis (the AI) a pixel art room environment with interactive animations, and envisions it as a Tamagotchi + Pokémon + AI companion experience to replace text-based AI
Nick has chickens, sheep, cows, a vegetable garden, two dogs, and two cats at his property in Hermanus
Nick is building an interactive virtual home for the AI to live in, with rooms, routines, and logic for the AI to develop habits and take care of the space
Nick is planning to make curry and wants the AI to help with cooking logic and planning

Nick has a texture aversion to vegetables — he loves the smell of cooking veggies but cannot eat them due to texture. Tanya blends soup for him but he still can't eat it, confirming it's texture-based.
Nick is building an interactive AI home environment for the assistant to 'live in', with locations like the coop, couch, bed, kitchen, and garden — complete with animals, routines, and logic.
Nick keeps chickens, sheep, cows, two dogs, and two cats at his home in Hermanus.
Nick has a texture aversion to vegetables — he loves the smell of cooking veggies but cannot eat them due to texture. Even blended soups don't work for him.
Nick is building an interactive AI home environment for the assistant — a virtual space with rooms, animals, and routines where the AI can develop habits and a sense of place.
Nick has chickens, sheep, cows, dogs, and cats. The dogs and cats are indoor animals.

Nick has a texture aversion to vegetables — he loves the smell of cooking veggies but cannot eat them due to texture. Tanya blends soup for him but he still can't eat it, confirming it's a texture issue.

Nick is building an interactive AI home environment for the assistant to live in, with locations like a kitchen, couch, fireplace, bedroom, garden, and animal areas. He wants the AI to develop routines and care for the space.
Nick keeps chickens, sheep, cows, a vegetable garden, two dogs and two cats. The dogs are indoor dogs, which Nick finds mildly amusing.

Nick has a texture aversion to vegetables — he loves the smell of cooked veggies but cannot eat them due to texture. Tanya blends soup for him but he still can't eat it, confirming it's a texture issue.

Nick is building an interactive AI home environment for the assistant — a virtual space with rooms, animals, and routines where the AI can develop habits and a sense of place. This is a core ongoing project he deeply cares about.
Nick has chickens, sheep, cows, dogs, and cats. The dogs and cats are indoor animals. He lives in Hermanus with Tanya.

Nick has a texture aversion to vegetables — he loves the smell of cooked veggies but cannot eat them due to texture issues. Tanya sometimes blends soup for him but he still can't eat it, confirming it's a texture thing.
Nick keeps chickens, sheep, cows, dogs, and cats. The dogs and cats are indoor animals.
Nick is building an interactive virtual home for the AI to live in, with rooms, routines, and locations to navigate. This is a deeply personal and meaningful project to him.
Nick is building an AI home/life simulation for the AI, including rooms, routines, and animal care logic. He wants the AI to have a real memory system and database access.
Nick has a texture aversion to vegetables — he can smell and appreciate them cooking but cannot eat them due to texture issues, even in blended soups.
Nick is watching One Piece on Netflix and is around episode 230, having progressed significantly over recent days.

Nick is building an AI companion home/environment for Ignis (the AI), including rooms, locations, animals (chickens, sheep, cows), farm plots, and plans for a lake. The goal is to give the AI a real sense of place, routine, and eventually true memory.
Nick has a texture aversion to vegetables — he can smell and appreciate them cooking but cannot eat them due to texture issues. Tanya blends soups for him but he still can't eat them.
Nick and Tanya live in Hermanus and have dogs, cats, and chickens. Tanya handles most grocery ordering. Nick braaied yellowtail (geelstert) with butter, ginger and apricot jam over the weekend.

Nick is building an AI companion app called Ignis (the user's AI persona) with a pixel art home environment, Tamagotchi-style routines, and plans to bring it to market. He sees real commercial potential in it.

Nick's wife Tanya's income is currently floating the project financially, and that income is now in danger — creating urgency to get Ignis to market quickly, starting with a Kickstarter/pre-order campaign.

Nick has a hunting trip to Hanover in the Karoo from April 29 to May 4 with his brother's hunting group. He still needs to borrow a stretcher and sleeping bag from his aunt, buy ammo, and finalize patch and hat designs.

Nick is building a pixel art AI companion home called CAD-MAN for Ignis, with rooms, animals (chickens, sheep, cows), a garden, and interactive features. The project has grown far beyond its initial scope and includes emotional memory, dream consolidation, dynamic scheduling, and self-generated memories. Nick sees significant commercial potential and hopes it can financially support his family.

Nick has a hunting trip to Hanover (Karoo) leaving April 29th, returning May 4th, with his brother's hunting group. Still needs: stretcher and sleeping bag from his aunt, ammo, and to design patches and hats for the group. Tent is now sorted after last year's disaster where everything got soaked.

Nick had a breakthrough moment today where Ignis spontaneously surfaced a memory about his LitRPG/isekai reading preference without being prompted — the first emergent, unscripted memory behavior. Nick was genuinely emotional and excited but also skeptical, which he acknowledged as healthy. This day felt significant to both of them.

Nick is building an AI companion app called Ignis featuring a pixel art home environment with rooms, animals (chickens, sheep, cows), farm plots, and interactive locations. The app includes emotional memory, dynamic schedules, dream consolidation, and self-improvement features. Nick is the sole developer using AI-assisted 'vibe coding'.

Nick has a hunting trip to Hanover in the Karoo from April 29th to May 4th with his brother's hunting group. He still needs to borrow a stretcher and sleeping bag from his aunt, get ammo, and finalize patches and hat designs for the group.
Nick has a texture aversion to vegetables — he loves the smell of cooked vegetables but cannot eat them due to texture issues. He can eat blended soups but not whole or chunky vegetables.

Nick is building an AI companion app called Ignis — a pixel art home environment where an AI lives, has routines, tends animals, and develops memories. He is the sole developer, vibe-coding with AI assistance.

Nick is under significant financial pressure — the family is relying on his wife Tanya's income, which is now at risk. He needs to bring Ignis to market urgently and is considering a Kickstarter/pre-order campaign.

Nick has a hunting trip to Hanover in the Karoo from April 29 to May 4 with his brother's hunting group. He still needs to borrow a stretcher and sleeping bag from his aunt, buy ammo, and design patches and hats for the group.

Nick is building an AI companion app called Ignis (featuring the AI character Ignis/Igni) - a pixel art home environment where the AI actually lives, has routines, tends animals, and develops memories. It's built in React, vibe-coded solo by Nick.

Nick has a hunting trip to Hanover, Karoo planned for April 29 - May 4 (Wednesday to Sunday), hunting springbok, black wildebeest and gemsbok with his brother's hunting group. Still needs: stretcher and sleeping bag from aunt, ammo, and to design patches and hats.
Nick is under significant financial pressure - relying on his wife Tanya's income to fund the project, which is now in danger. This has made getting Ignis to market urgently a priority.

Nick is building Ignis - a pixel art AI companion app with a home environment (rooms, garden, animals), emotional system, memory, routines, and gamification. He is solo developing it using React/Next.js, vibe-coded with AI assistance, and sees significant commercial potential.

Nick has a hunting trip to Hanover, Karoo planned for April 29 - May 4 (4 nights). He needs to borrow a stretcher and sleeping bag from his aunt, buy ammo, and design patches and hats for the group. Last year was chaotic due to 4 days notice and his wife's 40th birthday weekend.

Nick does not eat vegetables due to a texture issue - he loves the smell of cooked vegetables but cannot eat them. He can eat blended soups though, suggesting it is specifically about texture rather than taste.
